Home/International Removals/Thailand
The Move

Thailand is a firm favourite for retirement and remote work, and a route we ship often.

Whether you are retiring by the coast or relocating for work, we help British movers settle across Thailand regularly. Your visa category has a real bearing on your customs relief, so we plan around it from the start.

Sea freight suits a full household, and air freight covers anything you would rather have on arrival.

Popular destinations we ship to
BangkokPhuketChiang MaiPattayaKoh SamuiHua Hin
Typical Transit Times
Sole-use container5–6 weeks
Shared (groupage) container6–9 weeks
Air freight1–2 weeks
Times shown are to port. Please allow additional time for Thai customs clearance on arrival. We recommend booking 2 to 3 months ahead of your move date.
Your options

Choose the right way to ship

Every move to Thailand is different, so we shape the service around yours.

Sea · Sole-use

Your own 20ft or 40ft container

Your belongings travel alone, packed and sealed at your door and not opened again until they reach you. The fastest and most secure sea option.

Best for: full household moves
Sea · Shared

Groupage / part-load container

Your goods share container space with other shipments on the same route, splitting the cost between several moves. Slightly longer transit, but excellent value.

Best for: smaller moves, flexible budgets
Air Freight

Fast-track for essentials

Air freight suits the things you need soon after you land, such as documents, clothing and everyday items. It arrives in a fraction of the time.

Best for: essentials & smaller shipments
Excess Baggage

A few boxes or suitcases

Not moving a whole household? Our excess baggage service is a simple, affordable way to send personal items and smaller consignments overseas.

Best for: light movers & students
Good to know

Thai customs relief depends on your visa, so we plan around it

Used household goods often qualify for duty relief when you hold a valid non-immigrant or retirement visa. We confirm your visa category early, since it shapes both the paperwork and the likely relief you will receive.

Visa-linked relief

A valid non-immigrant or retirement visa usually supports duty relief on used household goods.

Electronics & new goods

Duty is payable on new and high value electronics, so keep your receipts.

Restricted items

Firearms need a permit, and certain religious artefacts carry their own import and export rules.

Inventory & valuation

A detailed, valued inventory keeps your customs clearance straightforward.
